intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visually present as healthy, identifiable plants. The environment is dominated by soil substrate and debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent/withered.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only dark, damp soil and a sensor probe. No plant matter.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot contains soil and a small, unidentified green filament (likely a remnant or weed).
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains the white rabbit anchor and soil. No Pothos foliage visible.
plant_audit.P4: Integrated into P2/P3 space; no distinct specimen identified.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots, suggesting recent irrigation or lack of evaporation.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (resembling crushed eggshells) on the surface of the black pot.
biome_observations.anomalies: A metallic spoon appears in the T-4 image and is subsequently removed. A white cylindrical object (possibly a marker or tool) appears in T-3 and T-2, then is removed.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-4: Introduction of a spoon and white cylindrical object; minor soil disturbance.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-1: Removal of external objects; soil remains static and devoid of plant life.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_current: No significant change; the biome remains in a dormant or failed state.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue in all pots indicates total systemic failure of the registered specimens. The soil appears saturated, which may have contributed to root rot or collapse.
anomalies: Presence of non-botanical objects (spoon, cylinder) in mid-sequence.
Persistent presence of white granular debris (eggshells) in the black pot.
Total absence of expected plant biomass.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Across the five-day sequence, the pots have transitioned from a state of potential neglect to total vacancy. The 'white rabbit' anchor remains, but the Pothos it was meant to accompany has vanished. The soil surface shows no signs of active growth, only stagnant moisture and scattered debris. The intervention of external tools (spoon/cylinder) suggests an attempt at maintenance or soil testing that failed to revive the specimens.
